w:r.w:t: Business cannot proceed without completing this step
Business can skip — can be completed later from profile settings
locked unless business has an active paid subscription
Business can select more than one option
Step 8 — Confirm Your Location on Map

This document defines every question asked during the NAAMPATA business sign-up flow, in the exact order they appear. Each step specifies the field type, whether it is required or optional, all available options, and implementation notes for the developer.
Questions are organised into logical steps — one topic per step. Do not mix unrelated questions on one screen.
Required fields must be completed before the business can advance to the next step.
Optional fields can be skipped and completed later from the business profile settings.
Steps with many options must use scrollable checkbox lists — never dropdowns for multi-select.
Progress indicator must be visible throughout the entire sign-up flow showing current step / total steps.
Every step must have a "Save & Continue" button and a "Back" button.
The entire flow must be completable on mobile with no horizontal scrolling.
All structured answers are stored in the database as structured data — not free text — for AI features and analytics.
The legal or trading name of the business, and a short tagline that describes what they do.
Text input. Max 150 characters. Optional. Helper text: "One sentence that describes what you do — e.g. Best Pizza in Lahore since 1998."
Text input. Title dropdown (Mr. / Ms. / Dr. / Other) + full name.

Business Name is used across the entire platform — search, profile, listings, broadcasts, and reviews. It must be entered accurately. Add a note: "Enter your business name exactly as customers know it."
Select all location types that apply to how your business reaches customers. You can select more than one.
Mobile Unit (Food Truck, Van, Pop-Up, etc.)
Store this as a multi-value enum array. Used for filtering (e.g. "Online businesses near me", "Home services available") and future AI categorisation.
What does your business primarily do?
Select the option that best describes the core nature of your business. Select all that apply.
We provide online or remote services
We rent out products, spaces, or equipment
We organise events, classes, or experiences
We operate as a marketplace or multi-vendor platform
Select the structure that best describes how your business delivers its products or services. Select all that apply.
What category best describes your business?
Select your primary business category and subcategory. The platform uses Google Business categories (4,000+ categories) as the base dataset. AI-powered suggestions will appear as you type.
. Searchable dropdown. AI suggests top 3 matches as business types. Source: Google Business categories dataset.
Optional. Appears after primary category is
Select all customer types your business sells to. This helps users find the right businesses and powers future filtering.
Enter the full address of your business. This is publicly displayed on your profile and used for radius-based search. Use the autocomplete to find your address quickly.
Address form with Google Places Autocomplete
Required. Dropdown — full countries list (all countries worldwide).
Required. Text input with Google Places Autocomplete (triggers after 3 characters, 300ms debounce, session tokens mandatory).
Required. Auto-filled from Places Autocomplete. Editable.
Optional. Field label and presence dynamically set by country (libaddressinput dataset). See Address Localisation Spec.
Optional. Field label dynamically set by country. Not shown for countries without postal codes (e.g. Pakistan, UAE). Format validated server-side.
Optional. Free text. Max 100 characters. Helper: "Floor number, building name, nearest landmark, etc."

Address fields are dynamically rendered based on country selection using the Google libaddressinput dataset (github.com/google/libaddressinput). Never hardcode field labels. Country selection drives the entire address form structure. See Address Form Spec for full implementation details.
Step 8 — Confirm Your Location on Map
Confirm your location on the map
After the address is entered, show a map with a pin at the detected location. The business can drag the pin to the exact position if needed. GPS auto-detect button also available.
Auto-place pin at the coordinates returned by the address geocoding queue (BullMQ).
Business can drag the pin to correct position if the auto-placement is inaccurate.
"Detect My Location" button — uses device GPS (when-in-use permission only, never at launch).
If GPS is denied: fallback to manual city selection, then manual pin drag.
Coordinates are stored permanently after registration. Geocoding API is never called again for the same business.
Duplicate location check runs here — if another business is within 10m with a similar name or phone, show a confirmation prompt (not a hard block).
Add your business contact details. These are displayed publicly on your profile. Multiple phone and WhatsApp numbers can be added, also mention with contact person name, position and title.
Required. International format with country code selector. Validated using libphonenumber (E.164 format, 8–15 digits). No duplicate country code entries.
Optional. Same validation as phone. Toggle: "Same as primary phone" to auto-fill.
Optional. Up to 5 named numbers on paid plan (each number can have a label e.g. "Sales", "Support", "WhatsApp Business").
Optional. Validated email format. Publicly displayed on profile.
Optional. Must begin with http:// or https://. Shown as clickable link on profile.
Phone number validation: use libphonenumber (free, open source). Remove the hardcoded 8-digit minimum. Support global formats (8–15 digits). Country code PK must not appear twice in the dropdown.
Set your regular opening hours for each day of the week. These power the real-time Open / Closed badge on your profile.
Toggle each day of the week on / off. Default: all days off.
Time picker (12hr or 24hr based on device locale) per active day.
If enabled, overrides all day/time settings and marks business as always open.
Auto-detected from country/city. Editable dropdown for correction.
Write a description of your business. This is displayed on your public profile and helps customers understand what you offer.

Min 20 characters, max 2,000 characters. Character counter shown. Rich text: bold, bullet lists, line breaks only (no images in description field). Helper: "Describe your services, experience, and what makes your business unique."
Multi-select. What languages does your business operate in? Helps users find businesses that serve them in their language.
Tell us a bit more about your business
These details build trust with customers and appear on your profile as trust badges.
Year picker dropdown. Range: 1900 to current year. Used to calculate and display "X years in business" badge on profile.
Toggle — Yes / No. If Yes: show info "Each branch must be registered as a separate listing." Informational only.
Where else can customers find you?
Optional. Must start with https://. Displayed as clickable link.
Optional. Validated as a facebook.com URL.
Optional. Validated as an instagram.com URL.
Optional. Label + URL. For platforms not listed above.
Show social media fields to all businesses during sign-up but lock them with an upgrade prompt for free plan users. This creates an incentive to upgrade without hiding the feature entirely.
Select all amenities and facilities available at your business location. These are shown on your profile and help customers choose where to go.
Does your business fall into any of these specialised sectors?
Add keywords that describe your business
Up to 10 keywords. Tag-style input — business types a keyword and presses Enter or comma to add it. Each keyword: max 40 characters. Show on free plan with upgrade prompt.
Show the keywords field on free plan with a lock icon and upgrade prompt. Do not hide it entirely — seeing the feature creates upgrade motivation.
Add common questions and answers about your business
FAQs are displayed on your public profile to he
Show locked with upgrade prompt. Do not hide.
Step 20 Does your business offer franchise or distribution opportunities?
Share expansion opportunities available to partners, dealers, and investors. This information is displayed on your public profile and enables B2B partner matching on NAAMPATA.
Franchise available in — select all that apply:
Franchise support provided — select all that apply:
Text input. Numeric entry + unit selector (sq ft / sq m). Optional. Helper: "Minimum floor area needed to operate this franchise."
Areas served — select all that apply:
Add your business logo and cover image
Your logo and cover image appear at the top of your business profile. All images are automatically optimised and delivered via CDN.
PNG or JPG. Max file size: 5MB. Auto-compressed to WebP. Helper text must show the recommended size.
Optional. Recommended: 1200×400px, PNG or JPG. Max file size: 10MB. Auto-compressed to WebP. Helper text must show the recommended size.
Optional. Up to 3 images on free plan. Unlimited on paid plan. Each max 10MB. Auto-compressed to WebP.
Business can group images into named albums with ordering control. Free plan: single gallery only.
Always show the recommended image dimensions as helper text directly below each upload field. Do not bury this information in a tooltip or help page. The business must see it before they upload.
Review and agree to our terms
The business must agree to all applicable terms before their listing is created. This step cannot be skipped.
Required checkbox. Link to full document. Cannot proceed without checking.
Required checkbox. Link to full document.
Required checkbox. Link to full document.
I confirm my business information is accurate
I consent to my business location being publicly displayed
Required checkbox. Location consent per legal requirements.
I agree to receive platform notifications and updates (optional)

Consent logging: at the moment the business checks each box and submits, record: UTC timestamp, IP address, device identifier, session ID, and version number of each document accepted. Retain for minimum 7 years. This is a legal requirement.
Listings are approved and go live automatically after sign-up is completed. There is no manual approval step. Remove the approval queue entirely.

After the listing goes live, the business is taken to their dashboard with a Profile Completion progress bar showing how complete their profile is. Incomplete optional sections show a prompt to complete them for a stronger profile score.
Profile score is calculated as a percentage and displayed on the business dashboard.
Higher profile completion = better search ranking (all else being equal).
Each incomplete optional section shows a "Complete now" button.
Businesses can edit any field from their profile settings at any time after registration.
Run duplicate detection during Step 8 (Map Pin) and again on final submission. Never hard-block — only prompt for confirmation.
Remove the first occurrence. Keep the second one only.
Payment Methods shown as a separate page
Remove the separate Payment Methods page. Payment methods are now part of Step 14 (Amenities) as checkboxes.
Service Mode shown as a separate page
Remove the separate Service Mode page. This is now covered by Steps 2, 3, and 14.
Remove the duplicate. Each country code must appear exactly once.
Replace with dynamic address form driven by country config (libaddressinput).
Remove. Use libphonenumber with E.164 validation instead.
Remove approval queue. All listings go live automatically on submission.
All structured question answers (Steps 2–6, 14, 15) must be stored as structured enum arrays in the database — never as free text strings.
This is required for AI categorisation, search filtering, analytics, and future feature development.
Each field must have a defined schema before development begins.
